title: #205 test(e2e)  setup complete end to end testing pipeline fix
body:
Findings
The repository had many unit/integration tests but lacked a real tests/e2e/ suite.
The code could not connect to local HTTP sandbox RPC endpoints because allowHttp was not enabled.
Real extension/restore transaction building could fail because SorobanDataBuilder was referenced incorrectly from rpc.
getCurrentLedger() was brittle when a sandbox RPC did not support getLatestLedger() correctly.
Fix Features Added
New E2E Framework
Created:
tests/e2e/
with:
tests/e2e/README.md
tests/e2e/helpers/in-memory-soroban-sandbox.ts
tests/e2e/sandbox-network.test.ts
E2E Lifecycle Covered
The new E2E test verifies:
local sandbox starts automatically
synthetic contract is deployed
Sorokeep watches the contract
TTL drops below threshold
monitor detects threshold crossing
auto-extension submits simulated ExtendFootprintTTLOp
TTL recovers
alert is resolved
extension history is recorded
RPC Client Fixes
Updated:
src/rpc/client.ts
Fixes include:
local HTTP RPC support
direct SorobanDataBuilder import
fallback from getLatestLedger() to getHealth()
New npm Script
Updated:
package.json
Added:
"test:e2e": "vitest run tests/e2e"
CLOSE #205

title: feat: recover from bad_sequence errors with sequence refresh and retry
body:
Closes #194

---


  feat: recover from bad_sequence errors with sequence refresh and retry
  
  Summary
  
  Adds automatic recovery from txBadSeq transaction failures in StellarRpcClient. When a sequence
  mismatch is detected on submission, the client refreshes the account sequence from the RPC and
  retries the transaction once.
  
  Changes
  
  - src/rpc/client.ts
    - Added isBadSeqError() private helper — decodes the errorResult XDR from a failed
  sendTransaction response and checks for the txBadSeq result code
    - submitExtension — on ERROR response, detects txBadSeq, logs a warning, re-fetches account
  sequence, and retries once
    - submitRestore — same recovery logic applied
  
  - tests/rpc/client.test.ts
    - Extended mock to support per-call sendTransaction responses and getAccount sequence cycling
    - Added 5 tests covering: successful recovery after txBadSeq (extension and restore), warning
  log on sequence correction, no retry on unrelated errors (extension and restore)
  
  What was tested
  
  All 232 tests pass. No regressions.
  
  Acceptance Criteria
  
  - ✅ Recovers and succeeds on resubmission after sequence mismatch
  - ✅ Logs sequence correction warnings
  
#194

title: feat: export watchContract and runMonitorCycle as public library API
body:
Export core functions as a public library API
  
  Adds a programmatic library entry point so third-party Node.js apps can import watchContract
  and runMonitorCycle directly from the sorokeep package without pulling in any CLI dependencies.
  
  Changes:
  
  - src/lib.ts — new library entry point re-exporting watchContract and runMonitorCycle (plus
  their TypeScript types) from the existing core modules
  - package.json — adds exports field mapping "." to dist/lib.js and dist/lib.d.ts, decoupling
  the library surface from the CLI entry point
  - tests/lib.test.ts — TDD tests verifying both symbols are importable and are functions
  
  Usage after this change:
  
  import { watchContract, runMonitorCycle } from "sorokeep";
  
  What was tested:
  
  - tests/lib.test.ts passes (2/2) confirming both exports resolve correctly
  - Build confirmed via npm run build — dist/lib.js and dist/lib.d.ts generated successfully
  - No existing passing tests were broken

closes #188

title: feat: add systemd service descriptor for daemon
body:
feat: systemd service descriptor for sorokeep daemon
  
  Adds everything needed to run the sorokeep monitoring daemon as a managed Linux system service.
  
  Changes
  
  - systemd/sorokeep-daemon.service — unit file with Restart=on-failure, journald logging, and
  clean SIGTERM shutdown
  - systemd/install-service.sh — one-command install script (copies unit file, reloads, enables,
  starts)
  - docs/systemd.md — setup, configuration, logging, and uninstall instructions
  - tests/systemd/service.test.ts — 18 TDD tests verifying all required directives
  
  Behaviour
  
  - Auto-restarts on crash with a 10s backoff (Restart=on-failure, RestartSec=10)
  - All stdout/stderr routed to journald (StandardOutput=journal, StandardError=journal),
  filterable with journalctl -u sorokeep-daemon
  - KillMode=mixed ensures the existing SIGTERM handler in the daemon triggers a graceful
  shutdown
  - User/Group and EnvironmentFile directives are included but commented out for easy production
  hardening
  
  Testing
  
  All 18 tests pass. Existing test suite unaffected.

closes #191

title: feat: add ChannelAccountPool for concurrent TTL extensions
body:
feat: Channel Account Pool for concurrent TTL extensions
  
  Summary
  
  Introduces a ChannelAccountPool that manages a set of pre-funded Stellar keypairs, enabling the
  daemon to submit multiple ExtendFootprintTTLOp transactions in parallel without hitting
  sequence number conflicts.
  
  Previously, runAutoExtensions processed contracts one at a time in a sequential loop — a single
  keypair was reused for every transaction, making concurrency impossible (each transaction
  requires a unique, incrementing sequence number per account).
  
  Changes
  
  New: src/core/channels.ts — ChannelAccountPool
  
  - acquire() — hands out a free channel slot in round-robin order; blocks callers via a Promise
  queue when all slots are busy
  - release() — returns a slot and unblocks the next waiter
  - getBalances() — returns cached XLM balances from DB
  - refreshBalances() — fetches native balances from the Stellar RPC and persists them;
  per-account failures are logged and swallowed
  
  Modified: src/core/extension.ts — runAutoExtensions
  
  - Replaced sequential for loop with Promise.all — all eligible contracts are now processed
  concurrently
  - Each task acquires a dedicated pool slot before submitting, released in finally
  - Falls back to the per-policy keypair if no channel accounts are registered (backward
  compatible)
  
  Modified: src/db/schema.sql + src/db/database.ts
  
  - New channel_accounts table with a live migration for existing databases
  
  Modified: src/db/repositories.ts
  
  - upsertChannelAccount, getChannelAccounts, updateChannelBalance, deleteChannelAccount
  
  New: tests/core/channels.test.ts
  
  - 16 tests written before implementation (TDD)
  
  Tests
  
  16 new tests — all passing
  243 total tests — all passing
  tsc --noEmit — clean
  
  Covers: pool construction, round-robin ordering, blocking behavior when all slots busy,
  distinct account assignment across concurrent tasks, overflow queuing (more tasks than
  accounts), balance reads, RPC balance refresh, graceful RPC failure, and all repository CRUD
  operations.
  
  How to register channel accounts
  
  Channel accounts must be pre-funded on-chain. Register them via the repository before starting
  the daemon:
  
  import { upsertChannelAccount } from "./src/db/repositories.js";
  
  upsertChannelAccount(db, {
    public_key: "GPUB...",
    keypair_source: "env:CHANNEL_KEY_1",
    network: "testnet",
  });

Closes #193

title: feat: add channels add/list/fund subcommands
body:
feat: add channels subcommands — add, list, fund
  
  Adds a sorokeep channels command group for managing channel accounts used for fee bumping and
  transaction parallelism.
  
  Subcommands:
  
  - channels add --key <publicKey> [--label] [--network] — registers a Stellar public key as a
  channel account
  - channels list [--network] — lists all registered channel accounts with funded status
  - channels fund --master-key <secretKey> [--amount] [--network] — sends XLM from a master
  wallet to all registered channel accounts in a single transaction
  
  Implementation:
  
  - New channel_accounts SQLite table (with live migration for existing databases)
  - sendPayments() on StellarRpcClient — builds a standard transaction with one PaymentOp per
  destination
  - Core logic in src/core/channels.ts, CLI in src/commands/channels.ts
  - 8 tests covering happy paths, duplicate key rejection, empty account guard, and error
  propagation
  
  Testing:
  
  All 8 new tests pass. Full suite: 235 tests across 14 files, 0 failures.

closes #196

title: test(e2e): verify monitoring daemon execution cycles
body:
Summary

Add end-to-end tests to validate monitoring daemon execution cycles, ensuring scheduled monitoring tasks run reliably, execute at expected intervals, and properly handle success, failure, and recovery scenarios.

Changes
Add E2E test suite for monitoring daemon lifecycle
Verify daemon startup and initialization behavior
Validate scheduled execution at configured intervals
Verify task completion and execution status reporting
Test daemon behavior across multiple execution cycles
Validate persistence of execution metadata and timestamps
Verify recovery after transient failures
Add assertions for logging and monitoring outputs
Improve test utilities for daemon orchestration and timing validation
Test Scenarios
Execution Cycle Validation
Daemon starts successfully
Scheduled jobs execute at expected intervals
Consecutive execution cycles complete successfully
Execution timestamps are updated correctly
Metrics and status information are recorded accurately
Failure Handling
Temporary task failures do not stop the daemon
Failed executions are logged correctly
Retry and recovery mechanisms function as expected
Subsequent cycles continue after failure recovery
Shutdown & Restart
Graceful daemon shutdown
Restart resumes monitoring operations correctly
No duplicate executions after restart
Execution state remains consistent across restarts
Expected Outcomes
Monitoring daemon executes tasks on schedule
Execution history reflects completed cycles accurately
Failure scenarios are handled without service interruption
Monitoring infrastructure remains stable under extended operation
Motivation

The monitoring daemon is responsible for critical background operations. Verifying execution cycles through end-to-end testing helps ensure long-running reliability, prevents scheduling regressions, and provides confidence that monitoring processes continue operating correctly in production environments.

Checklist
 Added E2E tests for daemon execution cycles
 Verified scheduled execution timing
 Added failure and recovery scenario coverage
 Added restart and shutdown validation
 Verified execution metadata and logging
 Improved test utilities for daemon orchestration
 Confirmed stable behavior across multiple execution cycles..closed #206

title: feat(costs): implement rolling average for cost anomaly detection
body:
Closes #167 

I have implemented a cost anomaly detection feature to monitor the resource consumption of automatic TTL extensions.

This was achieved by:

Establishing a Baseline: A new function was added to the database repository to calculate a rolling average of CPU and memory usage over the last 100 successful extensions for each contract.
Capturing Live Metrics: The RPC client was enhanced to capture the actual CPU instructions and memory bytes consumed by each new extension transaction.
Detecting Anomalies: The core auto-extension logic now compares the resource usage of each new transaction against the historical baseline. If the usage spikes more than 3x above the average, the transaction is flagged as an anomaly.
Logging and Recording: The anomaly status is saved to the extension_history table, and the daemon logs a clear warning message to alert operators of the potential cost issue.
